paste: We are seeking investigators studying cognitive decline in older adults, with particular interest in how genetic risk factors and vascular contributions interact to accelerate memory loss. Proposals should address early detection in patients who have not yet progressed to dementia.

This program supports work on hardening of the arteries and the buildup of plaque in blood vessel walls. We are especially interested in why some plaques rupture and cause sudden events while others remain stable for decades.

We are looking for collaborators who study what happens to heart muscle in the hours after a heart attack, including how the damaged tissue heals and scars, and why some patients go on to develop heart failure while others recover function.

Our foundation funds research on gut bacteria and how the microbes living in the intestine shape the immune system. We welcome proposals connecting these microbial communities to inflammatory bowel conditions.

This call seeks investigators working on wear and tear arthritis of the knee and hip, particularly the breakdown of joint cartilage with age and mechanical loading, and non-surgical approaches to slowing joint degeneration.

We support research into liver scarring driven by fatty liver disease, including the cellular events that turn ongoing inflammation into permanent scar tissue, and any imaging or blood-based approach that can track it without a biopsy.
We are interested in researchers studying long-lasting low back pain, especially why pain persists after the original injury has healed, and how the nervous system becomes sensitized over time.

This opportunity supports work on how the brain rewires itself after a stroke, including rehabilitation approaches that take advantage of the brain's ability to reorganize, and predictors of who will regain arm and hand function.
